text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is a closed book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: Using examples, compare and contrast the replication strategies used by positive sense single stranded RNA viruses and retroviruses.
How do viruses evade host immune responses?
Discuss how innate immune mechanisms support adaptive immune responses.
Discuss the immunological basis of transplant survival and rejection.
How do bacterial protein toxins and injected effector proteins contribute to host colonisation and direct damage during infection?
Compare and contrast the invasion mechanisms used by Toxoplasma and Plasmodium parasites to enter mammalian host cells.
Discuss the role of environmental factors in cancer development.
How does ischaemia damage cells and how is the damage reflected in morphological changes of cells and tissues?
